headline: Heat warning ends Tuesday — tip canes Wednesday morning.
weather_notes: Run both zones early Tuesday morning — 2 days dry entering 101°F; afternoon isolated storms (20% chance) aren't reliable enough to skip irrigation.
Limit all outdoor work to before 8 AM today — 102°F under an active Extreme Heat Warning that expires Tuesday at 6 AM MDT.
Check e1–e4 transplants Wednesday morning — first 96°F window after 4+ days above 100°F; pull any heat-damaged foliage before it invites disease.
Monitor new potato bag cutting daily — just 4 days since the Jul 9 restart, most vulnerable plant in continued 100°F+ heat.
Skip foliar sprays or feeds through Tuesday — 20–28% storm chance Tuesday–Wednesday could wash off applications within hours.

surfaced_tasks.title: Tip new blackberry primocanes if 3–4 ft tall
surfaced_tasks.reason: 3 days overdue; Wednesday's 96°F is the first bearable morning window after the Extreme Heat Warning lifts to get this done.

suggested_tasks.title: Harvest check — blackberry bed
suggested_tasks.description: Berries logged Jun 14; Triple Crown ripens mid-July — check for ready fruit before heat damages what's ready to pick.

suggested_tasks.title: Feed w1 strawberries with berry food
suggested_tasks.description: Last logged feeding was Jun 3 (40 days ago); apply Wednesday morning during the heat break to support continued production.
